opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Every time I stand up, the room goes completely black, my legs feel like jelly, and I have to sit back down instantly before I pass out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It has been happening for a couple of days and this morning I nearly fainted and collapsed back onto the bed when I got up. I'm frightened to walk around now.

symptom_narrative.character: everything goes black in front of my eyes and I go weak and jelly-legged the moment I stand up
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bad enough that this morning I nearly passed out and dropped back down onto the bed
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the dizziness is in my head; I also get a dull ache across the back of my neck and shoulders after I've stood up for a bit
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: only when I stand up from sitting or lying down; it settles within a few seconds once I sit or lie back down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting down or lying flat straight away
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up, especially getting out of bed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: legs feel like jelly, general weakness, and that ache across the neck and shoulders when I'm up
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before the last couple of days
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm too scared to move around so I've been staying in bed or the chair

past_medical_history.how_managed: I take a tablet for it, and the doctor recently put the dose up

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a tablet for my prostate (the dose was recently increased)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and is worried about falling and not being able to get up

objective_data_offered.value: when I sit and feel my pulse it seems steady, around 70
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-palpated resting pulse, not a device reading; cannot assess rhythm reliably
